rawText:
The writer of creative nonfiction occupies an inherently unstable ethical position, for she is simultaneously bound by a commitment to truthful representation and empowered by the narrative techniques — selection, compression, emphasis, the shaping of chronology into arc — that inevitably transform lived experience into something other than what it was.

This tension between documentary fidelity and narrative craft is not a problem to be solved but a condition to be inhabited, and the writers who navigate it most honestly are those who acknowledge, rather than conceal, the degree to which their art reshapes the reality it claims to record.

The question of consent is central yet irreducibly complicated: even when the subject of a biographical or testimonial work agrees to be written about, she cannot consent to the specific narrativization her life will undergo — the interpretive frame the writer will impose, the details that will be magnified or suppressed, the arc that will be discerned in what she experienced as formless contingency.

To write about another person is, in a fundamental sense, to exercise power over the terms in which that person will be understood, and this asymmetry between the writer who narrates and the subject who is narrated demands a form of ethical vigilance that no contractual agreement can fully discharge.

The use of composite characters — figures who combine traits of multiple real individuals into a single fictional persona — illustrates the ethical ambiguity at its sharpest, for the composite simultaneously claims the authority of verisimilitude and permits a degree of invention that blurs the boundary between what happened and what was imagined.

When the writer constructs a composite, she gains narrative efficiency and protects individual privacy, but she also creates an entity that exists in an ontologically uncertain space: real enough to carry the weight of testimony, yet fictional enough to evade the obligations of strict factual correspondence.

The right to privacy, often invoked as a counterweight to the writer's freedom, is itself a contested and culturally variable concept; what constitutes an unacceptable intrusion into private life differs enormously across legal systems, social norms, and historical periods, making it impossible to articulate a universal ethical standard for biographical writing that would satisfy all legitimate claims.

Yet the absence of a universal standard does not exempt the writer from ethical responsibility; it merely relocates that responsibility from the domain of codified rules to the domain of individual judgment, requiring each writer to conduct, in the silence of composition, a private reckoning with the potential consequences of disclosure.

The most ethically serious works of creative nonfiction tend to foreground their own epistemological limitations — acknowledging gaps in the record, confessing the writer's partiality, making visible the interpretive scaffolding that supports the narrative — thereby inviting the reader to receive the text not as transparent fact but as a mediated construction with its own biases and blind spots.

What biographical ethics ultimately demands is not the impossible standard of perfect objectivity but a practice of sustained attentiveness to the ways in which narrativization distributes dignity and harm: whose story is told, whose is silenced, whose complexity is honored, and whose is reduced to the flattening convenience of a supporting role.

The writer who takes these questions seriously will not necessarily produce a more comfortable text, but she will produce a more honest one — and in a genre that stakes its authority on truthfulness, honesty about the limits of one's own perspective may be the most important form of truth.

overview.title: The Ethics of Writing About Real People

sentences.text: Writers who tell true stories have a hard job because they must be honest but also make the story interesting.
sentences.translation: 실화를 쓰는 작가들은 정직해야 하면서도 이야기를 재미있게 만들어야 하기 때문에 힘든 일을 해요.

sentences.text: When you write about a real person, you change their life story just by choosing what to include and what to leave out.
sentences.translation: 실제 사람에 대해 쓸 때, 무엇을 포함하고 무엇을 빠뜨릴지 선택하는 것만으로도 그들의 인생 이야기를 바꾸게 돼요.

sentences.text: Even if someone agrees to be written about, they cannot control how the writer tells their story.
sentences.translation: 누군가가 자기에 대해 쓰는 것에 동의하더라도, 작가가 어떻게 이야기를 전하는지는 통제할 수 없어요.

sentences.text: Sometimes writers combine several real people into one character, which can be confusing about what is true.
sentences.translation: 때때로 작가들은 여러 실제 사람을 하나의 인물로 합치는데, 이것은 무엇이 사실인지 혼란스럽게 할 수 있어요.

sentences.text: Different countries and cultures have different rules about what is private.
sentences.translation: 다른 나라와 문화에는 무엇이 사적인 것인지에 대한 다른 규칙이 있어요.

sentences.text: Good writers of true stories admit that their version is not the whole truth but their best honest attempt.
sentences.translation: 실화를 잘 쓰는 작가들은 자신의 버전이 전체 진실이 아니라 최선의 정직한 시도라고 인정해요.

sentences.text: Creative nonfiction writers face an inherent ethical tension between truthful representation and the narrative shaping that transforms lived experience.
sentences.translation: 창작 논픽션 작가들은 진실한 재현과 삶의 경험을 변형하는 서사적 형성 사이의 내재적 윤리적 긴장에 직면해요.

sentences.text: Consent is complicated because subjects cannot agree in advance to the specific interpretive frame the writer will impose on their lives.
sentences.translation: 동의는 복잡한데, 피험자가 작가가 자신의 삶에 부과할 특정 해석적 틀에 미리 동의할 수 없기 때문이에요.

sentences.text: Composite characters blur the boundary between testimony and fiction, gaining narrative efficiency while creating ontological uncertainty.
sentences.translation: 합성 인물은 증언과 허구 사이의 경계를 흐리게 하며, 서사적 효율성을 얻는 동시에 존재론적 불확실성을 만들어요.

sentences.text: The right to privacy is culturally variable, making it impossible to establish a universal ethical standard for biographical writing.
sentences.translation: 프라이버시 권리는 문화적으로 가변적이어서 전기적 글쓰기를 위한 보편적 윤리적 기준을 수립하는 것이 불가능해요.

sentences.text: Ethically serious nonfiction foregrounds its own limitations, acknowledging gaps and making visible the interpretive scaffolding.
sentences.translation: 윤리적으로 진지한 논픽션은 자체의 한계를 전경화하며, 간극을 인정하고 해석적 비계를 가시화해요.

sentences.text: Biographical ethics demands attentiveness to how narrativization distributes dignity and harm — whose story is told and whose is silenced.
sentences.translation: 전기적 윤리는 서사화가 어떻게 존엄과 해를 분배하는지에 대한 주의를 요구해요 — 누구의 이야기가 전해지고 누구의 것이 침묵되는지.

sentences.text: This essay theorizes the ethical position of the creative nonfiction writer as inherently unstable, caught between documentary commitment and the transformative power of narrative technique.
sentences.translation: 이 에세이는 창작 논픽션 작가의 윤리적 위치를 기록적 헌신과 서사적 기법의 변형적 힘 사이에 포착된 본질적으로 불안정한 것으로 이론화해요.

sentences.text: The essay argues that consent is structurally inadequate because the subject cannot consent to narrativization itself — the interpretive frame, the selection of details, the imposition of arc on contingency.
sentences.translation: 에세이는 동의가 구조적으로 부적절하다고 주장하는데, 피험자가 서사화 자체에 — 해석적 틀, 세부사항의 선택, 우연성에 대한 서사적 호의 부과에 — 동의할 수 없기 때문이에요.

sentences.text: Composite characters are analyzed as ontologically liminal entities that simultaneously claim testimonial authority and fictional license, exposing the genre's foundational instability.
sentences.translation: 합성 인물은 증언적 권위와 허구적 허가를 동시에 주장하는 존재론적으로 경계적인 존재로 분석되며, 장르의 근본적 불안정성을 노출해요.

sentences.text: The essay rejects both universal ethical standards and ethical relativism, locating responsibility in the writer's individual judgment — a "private reckoning" conducted in the silence of composition.
sentences.translation: 에세이는 보편적 윤리적 기준과 윤리적 상대주의 모두를 거부하며, 책임을 작가의 개인적 판단 — 작문의 침묵 속에서 수행되는 "사적 계산" — 에 위치시켜요.

sentences.text: The essay identifies the most ethically serious nonfiction as that which foregrounds epistemological limitation, treating self-reflexivity as an ethical practice rather than a stylistic choice.
sentences.translation: 에세이는 가장 윤리적으로 진지한 논픽션을 인식론적 한계를 전경화하는 것으로 식별하며, 자기 반성성을 문체적 선택이 아닌 윤리적 실천으로 다뤄요.

sentences.text: The concluding formulation — that narrativization "distributes dignity and harm" — positions biographical ethics as a politics of representation with real consequences for whose humanity is recognized.
sentences.translation: 서사화가 "존엄과 해를 분배한다"는 결론적 공식은 전기적 윤리를 누구의 인간성이 인정받는지에 실질적 결과를 갖는 재현의 정치학으로 위치시켜요.

sentences.original: The writer of creative nonfiction occupies an inherently unstable ethical position, for she is simultaneously bound by a commitment to truthful representation and empowered by the narrative techniques — selection, compression, emphasis, the shaping of chronology into arc — that inevitably transform lived experience into something other than what it was.
sentences.translation: 창작 논픽션의 작가는 본질적으로 불안정한 윤리적 위치를 점유하는데, 그녀는 동시에 진실한 재현에 대한 헌신에 구속되면서도 서사적 기법에 — 선택, 압축, 강조, 연대기를 호로 형성하기에 — 의해 강화되며, 이 기법들은 불가피하게 삶의 경험을 그것이 원래 있었던 것과 다른 무언가로 변형한다.

grammarElements.text: The writer of creative nonfiction
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: occupies an inherently unstable ethical position
grammarElements.label: 동사 + 목적어

grammarElements.text: for she is simultaneously bound by a commitment to truthful representation
grammarElements.label: for 이유절 1 (수동태)

grammarElements.text: and empowered by the narrative techniques
grammarElements.label: 병렬 수동태

grammarElements.text: selection, compression, emphasis, the shaping of chronology into arc
grammarElements.label: 대시 삽입 사중 동격구

grammarElements.text: that inevitably transform lived experience into something other than what it was
grammarElements.label: 관계절

vocabulary.example: Creative nonfiction uses literary techniques to tell true stories.
Narrativization transforms raw experience into structured story.
The position is inherently unstable.

expressions.phrase: simultaneously bound by X and empowered by Y
expressions.meaning: 동시에 X에 구속되면서 Y에 의해 강화되는
expressions.usage: 한 주체가 상충하는 두 힘에 동시에 놓여 있음을 표현하는 역설적 병렬

expressions.phrase: the shaping of chronology into arc
expressions.usage: 시간적 사실을 서사적 구조로 변형하는 과정을 기술하는 문학 비평적 표현

patterns.pattern: [Agent] occupies an inherently unstable [position], for she is simultaneously bound by [commitment to X] and empowered by [techniques] — [list] — that inevitably transform [Y] into something other than what it was
patterns.explanation: 행위자의 윤리적 딜레마를 "simultaneously bound and empowered"의 역설적 병렬로 도입하고, 대시 삽입구로 기법을 열거하며, 관계절로 불가피한 변형을 선언하는 패턴.
examples: The documentary filmmaker occupies an inherently unstable ethical position, for she is simultaneously bound by a commitment to objective recording and empowered by the cinematic techniques — editing, framing, scoring, the sequencing of images into narrative — that inevitably transform witnessed events into something other than what they were.

drillQuestions.question: "Creative nonfiction" combines literary technique with _____ stories.
"Inherently unstable" means the instability is _____.
The writer is "bound" by truthfulness and "empowered" by _____.
"Chronology into arc" means turning _____ into story structure.

sentences.notes: 에세이의 핵심 역설을 도입하는 첫 문장. "simultaneously bound and empowered"가 창작 논픽션 작가의 이중적 위치를 역설적으로 표현하며, 사중 삽입구(selection, compression, emphasis, shaping)가 서사적 기법의 변형적 힘을 구체화한다.

koreanAlignment.en: The writer of creative nonfiction occupies an inherently unstable ethical position
koreanAlignment.ko: 창작 논픽션의 작가는 본질적으로 불안정한 윤리적 위치를 점유하는데

koreanAlignment.en: for she is simultaneously bound by a commitment to truthful representation
koreanAlignment.ko: 그녀는 동시에 진실한 재현에 대한 헌신에 구속되면서도

koreanAlignment.en: and empowered by the narrative techniques
koreanAlignment.ko: 서사적 기법에 의해 강화되며

koreanAlignment.en: selection, compression, emphasis, the shaping of chronology into arc
koreanAlignment.ko: 선택, 압축, 강조, 연대기를 호로 형성하기

koreanAlignment.en: that inevitably transform lived experience into something other than what it was
koreanAlignment.ko: 이 기법들은 삶의 경험을 원래와 다른 무언가로 불가피하게 변형한다

pronunciationNotes: nonfiction: /nɑːnˈfɪkʃən/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "논픽션"에 가깝다.
inherently: /ɪnˈhɪrəntli/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "인히어런틀리"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: This tension between documentary fidelity and narrative craft is not a problem to be solved but a condition to be inhabited, and the writers who navigate it most honestly are those who acknowledge, rather than conceal, the degree to which their art reshapes the reality it claims to record.
sentences.translation: 기록적 충실함과 서사적 기예 사이의 이 긴장은 해결해야 할 문제가 아니라 거주해야 할 조건이며, 이를 가장 정직하게 항해하는 작가들은 자신의 예술이 기록하겠다고 주장하는 현실을 재형성하는 정도를 감추기보다 인정하는 이들이다.

grammarElements.text: This tension between documentary fidelity and narrative craft
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: is not a problem to be solved but a condition to be inhabited
grammarElements.label: 보어 (not A but B)

grammarElements.text: and the writers who navigate it most honestly
grammarElements.label: 병렬절 주어 (관계절 수식)

grammarElements.text: are those who acknowledge, rather than conceal,
grammarElements.label: 보어 (rather than 대비)

grammarElements.text: the degree to which their art reshapes the reality it claims to record
grammarElements.label: 목적어 (이중 관계절)

vocabulary.example: The tension is a condition to be inhabited, not solved.
Writers must navigate the tension between truth and craft.
Documentary fidelity competes with narrative craft.

expressions.phrase: not a problem to be solved but a condition to be inhabited
expressions.meaning: 해결해야 할 문제가 아니라 거주해야 할 조건
expressions.usage: 해결 불가능한 딜레마를 수용해야 할 조건으로 재정의하는 존재론적 표현

expressions.phrase: acknowledge, rather than conceal
expressions.usage: "rather than" 삽입으로 정직한 접근과 은폐적 접근을 대비하는 표현

patterns.pattern: This tension between [X and Y] is not a problem to be solved but a condition to be inhabited, and the [agents] who [navigate] it most honestly are those who acknowledge, rather than conceal, the degree to which [their practice reshapes what it claims to Z]
patterns.explanation: "not a problem but a condition"으로 딜레마를 재정의하고, "those who acknowledge rather than conceal"로 정직한 실천의 기준을 제시하는 패턴.
examples: This tension between objectivity and subjectivity is not a problem to be solved but a condition to be inhabited, and the researchers who navigate it most honestly are those who acknowledge, rather than conceal, the degree to which their methodology shapes the evidence it claims to discover.

drillQuestions.question: "A condition to be inhabited" means the tension is _____.
"Navigate" here means to _____ through difficulty.
Honest writers _____ rather than conceal.
The art "reshapes the reality it claims to _____."

sentences.notes: "not a problem to be solved but a condition to be inhabited"가 에세이의 윤리적 입장을 존재론적으로 정립한다. 해결을 요구하는 것이 아니라 거주를 요구함으로써 딜레마의 영속성을 수용하며, "acknowledge rather than conceal"이 정직한 실천의 핵심 원칙을 제시한다.

koreanAlignment.en: This tension between documentary fidelity and narrative craft
koreanAlignment.ko: 기록적 충실함과 서사적 기예 사이의 이 긴장은

koreanAlignment.en: is not a problem to be solved but a condition to be inhabited
koreanAlignment.ko: 해결해야 할 문제가 아니라 거주해야 할 조건이며

koreanAlignment.en: and the writers who navigate it most honestly
koreanAlignment.ko: 이를 가장 정직하게 항해하는 작가들은

koreanAlignment.en: are those who acknowledge, rather than conceal
koreanAlignment.ko: 감추기보다 인정하는 이들이다

koreanAlignment.en: the degree to which their art reshapes the reality it claims to record
koreanAlignment.ko: 자신의 예술이 기록하겠다고 주장하는 현실을 재형성하는 정도를

pronunciationNotes: fidelity: /fɪˈdelɪti/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "피델리티"에 가깝다.
inhabited: /ɪnˈhæbɪtɪd/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "인해비티드"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: The question of consent is central yet irreducibly complicated: even when the subject of a biographical or testimonial work agrees to be written about, she cannot consent to the specific narrativization her life will undergo — the interpretive frame the writer will impose, the details that will be magnified or suppressed, the arc that will be discerned in what she experienced as formless contingency.
sentences.translation: 동의의 문제는 핵심적이면서도 환원 불가능하게 복잡하다: 전기적 또는 증언적 작품의 대상이 자신에 대해 쓰이는 것에 동의하더라도, 그녀는 자신의 삶이 겪을 특정한 서사화에 — 작가가 부과할 해석적 틀, 확대되거나 억압될 세부사항, 그녀가 형태 없는 우연성으로 경험한 것에서 식별될 서사적 호에 — 동의할 수 없다.

grammarElements.text: The question of consent
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: is central yet irreducibly complicated
grammarElements.label: 보어 (yet 대비)

grammarElements.text: even when the subject of a biographical or testimonial work agrees to be written about
grammarElements.label: 양보절

grammarElements.text: she cannot consent to the specific narrativization her life will undergo
grammarElements.label: 콜론 뒤 주절

grammarElements.text: the interpretive frame the writer will impose, the details that will be magnified or suppressed, the arc that will be discerned in what she experienced as formless contingency
grammarElements.label: 대시 삽입 삼중 동격구 (관계절 포함)

vocabulary.example: The subject cannot consent to the specific narrativization.
She experienced her life as formless contingency.
The subject of a testimonial work agrees to participate.

expressions.phrase: central yet irreducibly complicated
expressions.usage: 문제의 중요성과 해결 불가능성을 동시에 표현하는 양보적 표현

expressions.phrase: what she experienced as formless contingency
expressions.meaning: 그녀가 형태 없는 우연성으로 경험한 것
expressions.usage: 삶의 비서사적 경험을 기술하며 서사화와의 대비를 만드는 표현

patterns.pattern: The question of [consent] is central yet irreducibly complicated: even when [subject agrees], she cannot consent to [the specific process] — [the frame], [the details], [the arc] that will be [imposed on what she experienced as formless]
patterns.explanation: 동의의 구조적 한계를 밝히고, 대시 삽입 삼중 구체화로 서사화의 불가피한 변형을 열거하는 패턴.
examples: The question of informed consent in research is central yet irreducibly complicated: even when the participant agrees to the study, she cannot consent to the specific interpretation her data will undergo — the theoretical lens the researcher will apply, the patterns that will be highlighted or minimized, the conclusions that will be drawn from what she experienced as mere daily routine.

drillQuestions.question: "Consent" in this context involves agreeing to be _____ about.
"Formless contingency" describes life as _____ and shapeless.
"Narrativization" is the process of turning life into _____.
The "arc" is the _____ structure imposed on events.

sentences.notes: 동의(consent)의 구조적 한계를 분석하는 핵심 문장. "even when ... she cannot consent to"의 양보 구조가 표면적 동의와 실질적 동의의 격차를 드러내며, "formless contingency"가 삶의 비서사적 본질과 서사화의 변형적 힘 사이의 근본적 긴장을 포착한다.

koreanAlignment.en: The question of consent is central yet irreducibly complicated
koreanAlignment.ko: 동의의 문제는 핵심적이면서도 환원 불가능하게 복잡하다

koreanAlignment.en: even when the subject agrees to be written about
koreanAlignment.ko: 대상이 자신에 대해 쓰이는 것에 동의하더라도

koreanAlignment.en: she cannot consent to the specific narrativization her life will undergo
koreanAlignment.ko: 그녀는 자신의 삶이 겪을 특정한 서사화에 동의할 수 없다

koreanAlignment.en: the interpretive frame the writer will impose
koreanAlignment.ko: 작가가 부과할 해석적 틀

koreanAlignment.en: the arc that will be discerned in what she experienced as formless contingency
koreanAlignment.ko: 그녀가 형태 없는 우연성으로 경험한 것에서 식별될 서사적 호

pronunciationNotes: consent: /kənˈsent/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "컨센트"에 가깝다.
contingency: /kənˈtɪndʒənsi/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "컨틴전시"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: To write about another person is, in a fundamental sense, to exercise power over the terms in which that person will be understood, and this asymmetry between the writer who narrates and the subject who is narrated demands a form of ethical vigilance that no contractual agreement can fully discharge.
sentences.translation: 다른 사람에 대해 쓰는 것은 근본적인 의미에서 그 사람이 이해될 조건에 대한 권력을 행사하는 것이며, 서술하는 작가와 서술되는 대상 사이의 이 비대칭성은 어떤 계약적 합의도 완전히 이행할 수 없는 형태의 윤리적 경계를 요구한다.

grammarElements.text: To write about another person
grammarElements.label: 주어 (to 부정사구)

grammarElements.text: is, in a fundamental sense, to exercise power over the terms in which that person will be understood
grammarElements.label: 보어 (삽입구 + to 부정사구 + 관계절)

grammarElements.text: and this asymmetry between the writer who narrates and the subject who is narrated
grammarElements.label: 병렬절 주어 (이중 관계절)

grammarElements.text: demands a form of ethical vigilance that no contractual agreement can fully discharge
grammarElements.label: 동사 + 목적어 (관계절)

vocabulary.example: There is a fundamental asymmetry between writer and subject.
Biographical writing demands ethical vigilance.
No contract can fully discharge this ethical obligation.

expressions.phrase: to exercise power over the terms in which
expressions.usage: 글쓰기를 권력 행사로 정의하는 푸코적 표현

expressions.phrase: the writer who narrates and the subject who is narrated
expressions.usage: 능동과 수동의 대비로 권력 비대칭성을 구조적으로 표현하는 병렬

patterns.pattern: To [write about X] is, in a fundamental sense, to exercise power over [the terms in which X will be understood], and this asymmetry between [agent who acts] and [subject who is acted upon] demands [ethical vigilance] that no [agreement] can fully discharge
patterns.explanation: "To A is to B" 구조로 글쓰기를 권력 행사로 정의하고, 능동/수동 대비로 비대칭성을 구조적으로 표현하며, "no X can fully discharge"로 법적 해결의 한계를 선언하는 패턴.
examples: To photograph another person is, in a fundamental sense, to exercise power over the image in which that person will be remembered, and this asymmetry between the photographer who frames and the subject who is framed demands a form of ethical care that no model release can fully discharge.

drillQuestions.question: "Asymmetry" means a lack of _____ between two things.
"Ethical vigilance" means being constantly _____ about moral issues.
"Discharge" an obligation means to _____ it.
Writing about someone is described as exercising _____.

sentences.notes: 글쓰기를 권력 행사로 재정의하는 핵심 문장. "the writer who narrates and the subject who is narrated"의 능동/수동 대비가 구조적 비대칭성을 문법적으로 체현하며, "no contractual agreement can fully discharge"가 법적 해결의 불충분성을 선언한다.

koreanAlignment.en: To write about another person is, in a fundamental sense
koreanAlignment.ko: 다른 사람에 대해 쓰는 것은 근본적인 의미에서

koreanAlignment.en: to exercise power over the terms in which that person will be understood
koreanAlignment.ko: 그 사람이 이해될 조건에 대한 권력을 행사하는 것이며

koreanAlignment.en: and this asymmetry between the writer who narrates and the subject who is narrated
koreanAlignment.ko: 서술하는 작가와 서술되는 대상 사이의 이 비대칭성은

koreanAlignment.en: demands a form of ethical vigilance
koreanAlignment.ko: 윤리적 경계의 한 형태를 요구한다

koreanAlignment.en: that no contractual agreement can fully discharge
koreanAlignment.ko: 어떤 계약적 합의도 완전히 이행할 수 없는

pronunciationNotes: asymmetry: /eɪˈsɪmɪtri/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "에이시미트리"에 가깝다.
vigilance: /ˈvɪdʒɪləns/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "비질런스"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: The use of composite characters — figures who combine traits of multiple real individuals into a single fictional persona — illustrates the ethical ambiguity at its sharpest, for the composite simultaneously claims the authority of verisimilitude and permits a degree of invention that blurs the boundary between what happened and what was imagined.
sentences.translation: 합성 인물의 사용 — 여러 실제 개인의 특성을 하나의 허구적 인격에 결합하는 인물 — 은 윤리적 모호성을 가장 첨예하게 예시하는데, 합성 인물은 동시에 핍진성의 권위를 주장하면서 일어난 것과 상상된 것 사이의 경계를 흐리는 정도의 발명을 허용하기 때문이다.

grammarElements.text: The use of composite characters
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: figures who combine traits of multiple real individuals into a single fictional persona
grammarElements.label: 대시 삽입 동격구 (관계절)

grammarElements.text: illustrates the ethical ambiguity at its sharpest
grammarElements.label: 동사 + 목적어

grammarElements.text: for the composite simultaneously claims the authority of verisimilitude
grammarElements.label: for 이유절 1

grammarElements.text: and permits a degree of invention that blurs the boundary between what happened and what was imagined
grammarElements.label: 이유절 2 (관계절 + 이중 what절)

vocabulary.meaning: 합성 인물 (여러 실제 인물의 특성을 결합한 허구적 인물)
vocabulary.example: Composite characters blend multiple real people into one.
The composite claims the authority of verisimilitude.
Multiple traits are combined into a single fictional persona.

expressions.phrase: at its sharpest
expressions.usage: 문제가 가장 극명하게 드러나는 지점을 표시하는 최상급 표현

expressions.phrase: the boundary between what happened and what was imagined
expressions.meaning: 일어난 것과 상상된 것 사이의 경계
expressions.usage: 사실과 허구의 경계를 이중 what절로 기술하는 표현

patterns.pattern: The use of [technique] — [definition] — illustrates [the ambiguity] at its sharpest, for [it] simultaneously claims [authority of X] and permits [degree of Y] that blurs the boundary between [what happened and what was imagined]
patterns.explanation: 기법을 대시 삽입구로 정의하고, "simultaneously claims and permits"로 이중적 기능을 밝히며, 사실과 허구의 경계 흐림을 선언하는 패턴.
examples: The use of dramatic reenactment — scenes where actors perform documented events before the camera — illustrates the documentary's ethical ambiguity at its sharpest, for the reenactment simultaneously claims the authority of historical evidence and permits a degree of theatrical interpretation that blurs the boundary between what was recorded and what was performed.

drillQuestions.question: A "composite character" is made from _____ real people.
"Verisimilitude" means the appearance of being _____.
"At its sharpest" means at its most _____.
The boundary blurred is between fact and _____.

sentences.notes: 합성 인물(composite character)을 윤리적 모호성의 첨예한 사례로 분석한다. "simultaneously claims the authority of verisimilitude and permits invention"이 합성 인물의 이중적 기능 — 사실주의적 권위와 허구적 자유의 동시적 주장 — 을 정확히 포착한다.

koreanAlignment.en: The use of composite characters
koreanAlignment.ko: 합성 인물의 사용은

koreanAlignment.en: figures who combine traits of multiple real individuals into a single fictional persona
koreanAlignment.ko: 여러 실제 개인의 특성을 하나의 허구적 인격에 결합하는 인물

koreanAlignment.en: illustrates the ethical ambiguity at its sharpest
koreanAlignment.ko: 윤리적 모호성을 가장 첨예하게 예시하는데

koreanAlignment.en: for the composite simultaneously claims the authority of verisimilitude
koreanAlignment.ko: 합성 인물은 동시에 핍진성의 권위를 주장하면서

koreanAlignment.en: and permits a degree of invention that blurs the boundary between what happened and what was imagined
koreanAlignment.ko: 일어난 것과 상상된 것 사이의 경계를 흐리는 정도의 발명을 허용하기 때문이다

pronunciationNotes: composite: /kəmˈpɑːzɪt/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "컴파짓"에 가깝다.
verisimilitude: /ˌverɪsɪˈmɪlɪtjuːd/ — 네 번째 음절에 강세. "베리시밀리튜드"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: When the writer constructs a composite, she gains narrative efficiency and protects individual privacy, but she also creates an entity that exists in an ontologically uncertain space: real enough to carry the weight of testimony, yet fictional enough to evade the obligations of strict factual correspondence.
sentences.translation: 작가가 합성 인물을 구성할 때, 그녀는 서사적 효율성을 얻고 개인의 프라이버시를 보호하지만, 존재론적으로 불확실한 공간에 존재하는 실체를 또한 창조한다: 증언의 무게를 감당할 만큼 충분히 실재하면서도, 엄격한 사실적 대응의 의무를 회피할 만큼 충분히 허구적인.

grammarElements.text: When the writer constructs a composite
grammarElements.label: 시간 부사절

grammarElements.text: she gains narrative efficiency and protects individual privacy
grammarElements.label: 주절 (이중 동사)

grammarElements.text: but she also creates an entity that exists in an ontologically uncertain space
grammarElements.label: 대조절 (관계절)

grammarElements.text: real enough to carry the weight of testimony, yet fictional enough to evade the obligations of strict factual correspondence
grammarElements.label: 콜론 뒤 양보적 병렬 형용사구 (enough to A, yet enough to B)

vocabulary.example: The composite exists in an ontologically uncertain space.
The composite evades strict factual correspondence.
The composite is an entity in an uncertain ontological space.

expressions.phrase: an ontologically uncertain space
expressions.usage: 합성 인물의 사실도 허구도 아닌 존재론적 지위를 공간적 비유로 표현

expressions.phrase: real enough to X, yet fictional enough to Y
expressions.meaning: X하기에 충분히 실재하면서도 Y하기에 충분히 허구적인
expressions.usage: 양립 불가능한 두 속성의 동시적 소유를 표현하는 양보적 병렬

patterns.pattern: When [agent constructs X], she gains [benefit 1] and [benefit 2], but she also creates [an entity] that exists in [an uncertain space]: [real enough to A], yet [fictional enough to B]
patterns.explanation: 이중 이점을 제시한 뒤 "but also"로 윤리적 문제를 도입하고, 콜론 뒤 양보적 병렬로 존재론적 모호성을 기술하는 패턴.
examples: When the filmmaker stages a reenactment, she gains visual immediacy and emotional impact, but she also creates a scene that exists in an ontologically uncertain space: historical enough to carry the weight of documentary authority, yet theatrical enough to evade the obligations of strict evidential accuracy.

drillQuestions.question: "Ontologically" relates to the nature of _____.
The composite is in a space that is neither fully real nor fully _____.
"Carry the weight of testimony" means to be _____ enough to serve as evidence.
"Evade the obligations" means to _____ responsibility.

sentences.notes: 합성 인물의 존재론적 지위를 "ontologically uncertain space"로 명명한다. "real enough to carry testimony, yet fictional enough to evade obligations"의 양보적 병렬이 합성 인물의 근본적 모호성 — 증언과 허구의 사이 — 을 정밀하게 기술한다.

koreanAlignment.en: When the writer constructs a composite
koreanAlignment.ko: 작가가 합성 인물을 구성할 때

koreanAlignment.en: she gains narrative efficiency and protects individual privacy
koreanAlignment.ko: 그녀는 서사적 효율성을 얻고 개인의 프라이버시를 보호하지만

koreanAlignment.en: but she also creates an entity that exists in an ontologically uncertain space
koreanAlignment.ko: 존재론적으로 불확실한 공간에 존재하는 실체를 또한 창조한다

koreanAlignment.en: real enough to carry the weight of testimony
koreanAlignment.ko: 증언의 무게를 감당할 만큼 충분히 실재하면서도

koreanAlignment.en: yet fictional enough to evade the obligations of strict factual correspondence
koreanAlignment.ko: 엄격한 사실적 대응의 의무를 회피할 만큼 충분히 허구적인

pronunciationNotes: ontologically: /ˌɑːntəˈlɑːdʒɪkli/ — 세 번째 음절에 강세. "안톨라지클리"에 가깝다.
verisimilitude: /ˌverɪsɪˈmɪlɪtjuːd/ — 네 번째 음절에 강세. "베리시밀리튜드"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: The right to privacy, often invoked as a counterweight to the writer's freedom, is itself a contested and culturally variable concept; what constitutes an unacceptable intrusion into private life differs enormously across legal systems, social norms, and historical periods, making it impossible to articulate a universal ethical standard for biographical writing that would satisfy all legitimate claims.
sentences.translation: 프라이버시 권리는, 종종 작가의 자유에 대한 균형추로 원용되는데, 그 자체가 논쟁적이고 문화적으로 가변적인 개념이다; 사생활에 대한 용납할 수 없는 침입을 구성하는 것은 법 체계, 사회적 규범, 역사적 시기에 따라 크게 다르며, 모든 정당한 주장을 만족시킬 전기적 글쓰기를 위한 보편적 윤리적 기준을 명확히 하는 것을 불가능하게 만든다.

grammarElements.text: The right to privacy, often invoked as a counterweight to the writer's freedom,
grammarElements.label: 주어 (삽입 분사구문)

grammarElements.text: is itself a contested and culturally variable concept
grammarElements.label: 보어

grammarElements.text: what constitutes an unacceptable intrusion into private life
grammarElements.label: 세미콜론 뒤 what절 주어

grammarElements.text: differs enormously across legal systems, social norms, and historical periods
grammarElements.label: 동사 + 삼중 전치사구

grammarElements.text: making it impossible to articulate a universal ethical standard for biographical writing that would satisfy all legitimate claims
grammarElements.label: 분사구문 (가목적어 it + to 부정사)

vocabulary.example: Privacy is invoked as a counterweight to the writer's freedom.
Privacy is a contested concept.
What constitutes an unacceptable intrusion into private life varies.

expressions.phrase: often invoked as a counterweight to
expressions.usage: 한 원칙이 다른 원칙에 대항하여 동원되는 상황을 기술하는 법적·학술적 표현

expressions.phrase: differs enormously across
expressions.usage: 문화적·역사적 가변성을 강조하는 비교 표현

patterns.pattern: [The right to X], often invoked as a counterweight to [Y], is itself a contested and culturally variable concept; what constitutes [violation] differs enormously across [A, B, and C], making it impossible to articulate a universal [standard] that would satisfy all [claims]
patterns.explanation: 권리를 논쟁적 개념으로 재정의하고, 문화적 가변성을 삼중 예시로 증명하며, 보편적 기준의 불가능성을 분사구문으로 선언하는 패턴.
examples: The right to free speech, often invoked as a counterweight to community standards, is itself a contested and culturally variable concept; what constitutes unacceptable expression differs enormously across political systems, religious traditions, and generational attitudes, making it impossible to articulate a universal ethical standard for public discourse that would satisfy all legitimate interests.

drillQuestions.question: A "counterweight" serves to _____ another force.
"Contested" means the concept is _____.
"Intrusion" means an unwanted _____ into privacy.
The impossibility is of a _____ ethical standard.

sentences.notes: 프라이버시 권리의 문화적 가변성을 분석하여 보편적 윤리 기준의 불가능성을 논증한다. "contested and culturally variable"가 권리의 본질적 불안정성을 밝히며, 삼중 변수(법 체계, 사회적 규범, 역사적 시기)가 가변성의 다차원적 원천을 열거한다.

koreanAlignment.en: The right to privacy, often invoked as a counterweight to the writer's freedom
koreanAlignment.ko: 프라이버시 권리는 종종 작가의 자유에 대한 균형추로 원용되는데

koreanAlignment.en: is itself a contested and culturally variable concept
koreanAlignment.ko: 그 자체가 논쟁적이고 문화적으로 가변적인 개념이다

koreanAlignment.en: what constitutes an unacceptable intrusion into private life
koreanAlignment.ko: 사생활에 대한 용납할 수 없는 침입을 구성하는 것은

koreanAlignment.en: differs enormously across legal systems, social norms, and historical periods
koreanAlignment.ko: 법 체계, 사회적 규범, 역사적 시기에 따라 크게 다르며

koreanAlignment.en: making it impossible to articulate a universal ethical standard
koreanAlignment.ko: 보편적 윤리적 기준을 명확히 하는 것을 불가능하게 만든다

pronunciationNotes: counterweight: /ˈkaʊntərweɪt/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "카운터웨이트"에 가깝다.
intrusion: /ɪnˈtruːʒən/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "인트루전"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: Yet the absence of a universal standard does not exempt the writer from ethical responsibility; it merely relocates that responsibility from the domain of codified rules to the domain of individual judgment, requiring each writer to conduct, in the silence of composition, a private reckoning with the potential consequences of disclosure.
sentences.translation: 그러나 보편적 기준의 부재가 작가를 윤리적 책임에서 면제하는 것은 아니다; 그것은 단지 그 책임을 성문화된 규칙의 영역에서 개인적 판단의 영역으로 재배치할 뿐이며, 각 작가가 작문의 침묵 속에서 공개의 잠재적 결과에 대한 사적 계산을 수행하도록 요구한다.

grammarElements.text: Yet the absence of a universal standard
grammarElements.label: 주어 (Yet 전환)

grammarElements.text: does not exempt the writer from ethical responsibility
grammarElements.label: 동사 (부정)

grammarElements.text: it merely relocates that responsibility from the domain of codified rules to the domain of individual judgment
grammarElements.label: 세미콜론 뒤 설명절 (from A to B)

grammarElements.text: requiring each writer to conduct, in the silence of composition, a private reckoning with the potential consequences of disclosure
grammarElements.label: 분사구문 (삽입 부사구)

vocabulary.example: The responsibility moves beyond codified rules.
The writer conducts a private reckoning with consequences.
The writer must consider the consequences of disclosure.

expressions.phrase: does not exempt X from Y
expressions.usage: 부재가 면책을 의미하지 않음을 선언하는 법적·윤리적 표현

expressions.phrase: in the silence of composition
expressions.usage: 글쓰기 행위의 고독하고 내면적인 성격을 시적으로 표현

patterns.pattern: Yet the absence of [a universal standard] does not exempt [agent] from [responsibility]; it merely relocates [responsibility] from [codified rules] to [individual judgment], requiring each [agent] to conduct, in the [silence of practice], a private reckoning with [consequences]
patterns.explanation: "Yet"으로 상대주의적 오독을 차단하고, "merely relocates"로 책임의 재배치를 기술하며, "in the silence of"라는 시적 삽입구로 개인적 윤리의 고독한 성격을 강조하는 패턴.
examples: Yet the absence of a universal aesthetic standard does not exempt the artist from critical responsibility; it merely relocates that responsibility from the domain of received conventions to the domain of personal conviction, requiring each artist to conduct, in the solitude of the studio, a private reckoning with the potential implications of her formal choices.

drillQuestions.question: "Codified" means written into formal _____.
"Reckoning" here means a serious _____ of consequences.
"In the silence of composition" emphasizes writing's _____ nature.
Responsibility is "relocated," not _____.

sentences.notes: "Yet"이 상대주의적 오독을 선제적으로 차단한다. "merely relocates from ... to"가 책임의 소멸이 아닌 재배치를 정확히 기술하며, "in the silence of composition"이라는 시적 삽입구가 작가의 윤리적 판단의 고독하고 내면적인 성격을 감동적으로 표현한다.

koreanAlignment.en: Yet the absence of a universal standard does not exempt the writer from ethical responsibility
koreanAlignment.ko: 그러나 보편적 기준의 부재가 작가를 윤리적 책임에서 면제하는 것은 아니다

koreanAlignment.en: it merely relocates that responsibility from the domain of codified rules to the domain of individual judgment
koreanAlignment.ko: 그것은 단지 그 책임을 성문화된 규칙의 영역에서 개인적 판단의 영역으로 재배치할 뿐이며

koreanAlignment.en: requiring each writer to conduct, in the silence of composition
koreanAlignment.ko: 각 작가가 작문의 침묵 속에서

koreanAlignment.en: a private reckoning with the potential consequences of disclosure
koreanAlignment.ko: 공개의 잠재적 결과에 대한 사적 계산을 수행하도록 요구한다

pronunciationNotes: codified: /ˈkɑːdɪfaɪd/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "카디파이드"에 가깝다.
reckoning: /ˈrekənɪŋ/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "레커닝"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: The most ethically serious works of creative nonfiction tend to foreground their own epistemological limitations — acknowledging gaps in the record, confessing the writer's partiality, making visible the interpretive scaffolding that supports the narrative — thereby inviting the reader to receive the text not as transparent fact but as a mediated construction with its own biases and blind spots.
sentences.translation: 가장 윤리적으로 진지한 창작 논픽션 작품들은 자체의 인식론적 한계를 전경화하는 경향이 있다 — 기록의 간극을 인정하고, 작가의 편파성을 고백하며, 서사를 지지하는 해석적 비계를 가시화한다 — 이로써 독자가 텍스트를 투명한 사실이 아니라 자체의 편견과 맹점을 가진 매개된 구성물로 수용하도록 초대한다.

grammarElements.text: The most ethically serious works of creative nonfiction
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: tend to foreground their own epistemological limitations
grammarElements.label: 동사

grammarElements.text: acknowledging gaps in the record, confessing the writer's partiality, making visible the interpretive scaffolding that supports the narrative
grammarElements.label: 대시 삽입 삼중 분사구문

grammarElements.text: thereby inviting the reader to receive the text not as transparent fact but as a mediated construction with its own biases and blind spots
grammarElements.label: 결과 분사구문 (not as A but as B)

vocabulary.example: Ethical nonfiction foregrounds its own limitations.
The interpretive scaffolding supporting the narrative is made visible.
The writer confesses her own partiality.

expressions.phrase: foreground their own epistemological limitations
expressions.usage: 자기 반성적 텍스트의 핵심 특성을 기술하는 문학 비평적 표현

expressions.phrase: a mediated construction with its own biases and blind spots
expressions.meaning: 자체의 편견과 맹점을 가진 매개된 구성물
expressions.usage: 텍스트의 비투명성을 인정하는 구성주의적 표현

patterns.pattern: The most [ethically serious] works tend to foreground their own [limitations] — [acknowledging A, confessing B, making visible C] — thereby inviting the reader to receive the text not as [transparent fact] but as [a mediated construction]
patterns.explanation: 윤리적 모범을 제시하고, 삼중 분사구문으로 전경화의 구체적 방법을 열거하며, "not as A but as B"로 독자의 수용 태도를 재규정하는 패턴.
examples: The most intellectually honest histories tend to foreground their own methodological limitations — acknowledging archival gaps, confessing the historian's ideological commitments, making visible the interpretive frameworks that organize the evidence — thereby inviting the reader to receive the narrative not as objective truth but as a disciplined construction with its own perspectives and exclusions.

drillQuestions.question: "Foreground" means to bring to the _____.
"Scaffolding" is a _____ that supports something being built.
"Partiality" means the writer is not completely _____.
The text should be received as a "mediated _____."

sentences.notes: 윤리적 모범을 자기 반성적 글쓰기로 제시하는 문장. "foreground their own epistemological limitations"가 핵심 원칙을 선언하고, 삼중 분사구문(acknowledging, confessing, making visible)이 전경화의 구체적 실천을 열거한다. "interpretive scaffolding"의 건축적 비유가 서사의 구성적 본질을 효과적으로 표현한다.

koreanAlignment.en: The most ethically serious works of creative nonfiction tend to foreground their own epistemological limitations
koreanAlignment.ko: 가장 윤리적으로 진지한 창작 논픽션 작품들은 자체의 인식론적 한계를 전경화하는 경향이 있다

koreanAlignment.en: acknowledging gaps in the record, confessing the writer's partiality
koreanAlignment.ko: 기록의 간극을 인정하고 작가의 편파성을 고백하며

koreanAlignment.en: making visible the interpretive scaffolding that supports the narrative
koreanAlignment.ko: 서사를 지지하는 해석적 비계를 가시화한다

koreanAlignment.en: thereby inviting the reader to receive the text not as transparent fact
koreanAlignment.ko: 이로써 독자가 텍스트를 투명한 사실이 아니라

koreanAlignment.en: but as a mediated construction with its own biases and blind spots
koreanAlignment.ko: 자체의 편견과 맹점을 가진 매개된 구성물로 수용하도록 초대한다

pronunciationNotes: scaffolding: /ˈskæfəldɪŋ/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "스캐폴딩"에 가깝다.
partiality: /ˌpɑːrʃiˈælɪti/ — 세 번째 음절에 강세. "파셜리티"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: What biographical ethics ultimately demands is not the impossible standard of perfect objectivity but a practice of sustained attentiveness to the ways in which narrativization distributes dignity and harm: whose story is told, whose is silenced, whose complexity is honored, and whose is reduced to the flattening convenience of a supporting role.
sentences.translation: 전기적 윤리가 궁극적으로 요구하는 것은 완벽한 객관성이라는 불가능한 기준이 아니라, 서사화가 존엄과 해를 분배하는 방식에 대한 지속적 주의의 실천이다: 누구의 이야기가 전해지고, 누구의 것이 침묵되며, 누구의 복잡성이 존중되고, 누구의 것이 조연의 납작한 편의로 축소되는지.

grammarElements.text: What biographical ethics ultimately demands
grammarElements.label: 주어 (what 명사절)

grammarElements.text: is not the impossible standard of perfect objectivity
grammarElements.label: 부정 보어

grammarElements.text: but a practice of sustained attentiveness to the ways in which narrativization distributes dignity and harm
grammarElements.label: 대조 보어 (but)

grammarElements.text: whose story is told, whose is silenced, whose complexity is honored, and whose is reduced to the flattening convenience of a supporting role
grammarElements.label: 콜론 뒤 사중 병렬 의문절

vocabulary.example: Narrativization distributes dignity and harm.
Ethics demands sustained attentiveness.
A supporting role reduces complexity to flattening convenience.

expressions.phrase: distributes dignity and harm
expressions.usage: 서사화를 정의(justice)의 문제로 재구성하는 핵심적 표현

expressions.phrase: the flattening convenience of a supporting role
expressions.usage: 인물의 복잡성이 서사적 편의를 위해 축소되는 것을 비판하는 표현

patterns.pattern: What [ethics] ultimately demands is not [impossible standard] but a practice of [sustained attentiveness] to [how X distributes Y and Z]: whose [A] is [done], whose is [not done], whose [B] is [honored], and whose is [reduced]
patterns.explanation: "not A but B"로 윤리적 요구를 재정의하고, 콜론 뒤 사중 "whose" 병렬로 분배적 정의의 구체적 질문을 제기하는 패턴.
examples: What educational ethics ultimately demands is not the impossible standard of perfect equality but a practice of sustained attentiveness to the ways in which pedagogy distributes opportunity and disadvantage: whose potential is nurtured, whose is overlooked, whose voice is amplified, and whose is reduced to the silent compliance of a passive learner.

drillQuestions.question: "Distributes dignity and harm" frames narrativization as a matter of _____.
"Sustained attentiveness" means ongoing careful _____.
"Flattening" means reducing _____ to simplicity.
The four "whose" questions address the _____ of representation.

sentences.notes: 에세이의 핵심 윤리적 요구를 "distributes dignity and harm"으로 공식화한다. 사중 "whose" 병렬(told/silenced, honored/reduced)이 서사화의 분배적 정의 차원을 체계적으로 질문하며, "the flattening convenience of a supporting role"이 인물의 복잡성 축소를 비판적으로 표현한다.

koreanAlignment.en: What biographical ethics ultimately demands
koreanAlignment.ko: 전기적 윤리가 궁극적으로 요구하는 것은

koreanAlignment.en: is not the impossible standard of perfect objectivity
koreanAlignment.ko: 완벽한 객관성이라는 불가능한 기준이 아니라

koreanAlignment.en: but a practice of sustained attentiveness to the ways in which narrativization distributes dignity and harm
koreanAlignment.ko: 서사화가 존엄과 해를 분배하는 방식에 대한 지속적 주의의 실천이다

koreanAlignment.en: whose story is told, whose is silenced
koreanAlignment.ko: 누구의 이야기가 전해지고, 누구의 것이 침묵되며

koreanAlignment.en: whose complexity is honored, and whose is reduced to the flattening convenience of a supporting role
koreanAlignment.ko: 누구의 복잡성이 존중되고, 누구의 것이 조연의 납작한 편의로 축소되는지

pronunciationNotes: narrativization: /ˌnærətɪvaɪˈzeɪʃən/ — 다섯 번째 음절에 강세. "내러티바이제이션"에 가깝다.
attentiveness: /əˈtentɪvnəs/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "어텐티브니스"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: The writer who takes these questions seriously will not necessarily produce a more comfortable text, but she will produce a more honest one — and in a genre that stakes its authority on truthfulness, honesty about the limits of one's own perspective may be the most important form of truth.
sentences.translation: 이 질문들을 진지하게 받아들이는 작가가 반드시 더 편안한 텍스트를 생산하는 것은 아니지만, 더 정직한 텍스트를 생산할 것이다 — 그리고 진실성에 자신의 권위를 건 장르에서, 자신의 관점의 한계에 대한 정직함이 가장 중요한 형태의 진실일 수 있다.

grammarElements.text: The writer who takes these questions seriously
grammarElements.label: 주어 (관계절 수식)

grammarElements.text: will not necessarily produce a more comfortable text
grammarElements.label: 동사 (부정)

grammarElements.text: but she will produce a more honest one
grammarElements.label: 대조절

grammarElements.text: and in a genre that stakes its authority on truthfulness
grammarElements.label: 대시 뒤 전치사구 (관계절 포함)

grammarElements.text: honesty about the limits of one's own perspective may be the most important form of truth
grammarElements.label: 결론절

vocabulary.example: The genre stakes its authority on truthfulness.
Creative nonfiction stakes its authority on truthfulness.
Honesty about the limits of one's own perspective is essential.

expressions.phrase: not necessarily a more comfortable text, but a more honest one
expressions.meaning: 반드시 더 편안한 텍스트가 아니라 더 정직한 텍스트
expressions.usage: 편안함과 정직함의 대비로 윤리적 글쓰기의 불편한 성격을 인정하는 표현

expressions.phrase: honesty about the limits of one's own perspective may be the most important form of truth
expressions.meaning: 자신의 관점의 한계에 대한 정직함이 가장 중요한 형태의 진실일 수 있다
expressions.usage: 메타-정직함을 최고의 진실로 정의하는 역설적 결론

patterns.pattern: The [agent] who takes these questions seriously will not necessarily produce a more [comfortable X], but she will produce a more [honest] one — and in a [domain] that stakes its authority on [value], [meta-honesty] may be the most important form of [that value]
patterns.explanation: 편안함과 정직함의 대비로 윤리적 실천의 불편한 성격을 인정하고, 대시 뒤 결론문으로 메타-정직함을 최고의 진실로 정의하는 마무리 패턴.
examples: The researcher who takes these questions seriously will not necessarily produce a more conclusive study, but she will produce a more rigorous one — and in a discipline that stakes its authority on empirical evidence, rigor about the limits of one's own methodology may be the most important form of scientific integrity.

drillQuestions.question: "Stakes its authority" means the genre _____ its credibility on something.
The text produced may not be _____ but it will be honest.
"Honesty about limits" is described as a form of _____.
The conclusion is that meta-honesty may be the _____ form of truth.

sentences.notes: 에세이의 마무리 역설. "not necessarily more comfortable but more honest"가 윤리적 글쓰기의 불편한 성격을 수용하며, "honesty about the limits of one's own perspective may be the most important form of truth"가 메타-정직함을 진실의 최고 형태로 격상시키는 역설적 결론을 형성한다. 진실성에 권위를 건 장르에서 한계의 인정이 곧 진실이라는 통찰로 에세이를 마무리한다.

koreanAlignment.en: The writer who takes these questions seriously
koreanAlignment.ko: 이 질문들을 진지하게 받아들이는 작가가

koreanAlignment.en: will not necessarily produce a more comfortable text
koreanAlignment.ko: 반드시 더 편안한 텍스트를 생산하는 것은 아니지만

koreanAlignment.en: but she will produce a more honest one
koreanAlignment.ko: 더 정직한 텍스트를 생산할 것이다

koreanAlignment.en: and in a genre that stakes its authority on truthfulness
koreanAlignment.ko: 진실성에 자신의 권위를 건 장르에서

koreanAlignment.en: honesty about the limits of one's own perspective may be the most important form of truth
koreanAlignment.ko: 자신의 관점의 한계에 대한 정직함이 가장 중요한 형태의 진실일 수 있다

pronunciationNotes: truthfulness: /ˈtruːθfəlnəs/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "트루스풀니스"에 가깝다.
perspective: /pərˈspektɪv/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "퍼스펙티브"에 가깝다.
